moments.name: PPE & Safety Requirements
moments.description: Introduction to required personal protective equipment and safety protocols for EAF steel tapping operations.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Welcome to the Electric Arc Furnace steel tapping procedure training. Before we begin, let's review the critical personal protective equipment required for this operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: TextMediaAction
actions.Data:
{"content":"Required PPE:
• Heat-resistant safety hat
• Fire retardant uniform
• Aluminized gloves
• Safety shoes
• Face shield
• Anti-fog safety glasses
• Ear plugs
• Dust mask (N95)","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You must wear a heat-resistant safety hat, fire retardant uniform, aluminized gloves, and safety shoes. Additionally, use a face shield, anti-fog safety glasses, ear plugs, and an N95 dust mask at all times during tapping operations.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Ensure your two-way radio is functional for communication with the control room and crane operator. Never proceed without complete PPE and working communication equipment.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now that you understand the safety requirements, let's move on to verifying the furnace readiness for tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel production facility with player standing at safe observation point viewing PPE equipment checklist overlay in virtual environment.

moments.name: Ladle Preparation
moments.description: Narration explaining the importance of ladle preheating and inspection before tapping operations.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Before we begin the tapping process, proper ladle preparation is critical. The ladle must be preheated to prevent thermal shock and carefully inspected for any structural damage or refractory wear. A properly prepared ladle ensures safe handling of molten steel and maintains product quality.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Verify that the ladle refractory lining is intact, check for any cracks or erosion, and confirm that all drainage points are clear. The ladle should be positioned correctly under the furnace tapping spout using the overhead crane system.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"With the ladle properly prepared and positioned, we can now proceed to the furnace preparation and tapping procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel mill environment showing a large refractory-lined ladle positioned near an electric arc furnace, with ambient lighting highlighting the ladle's interior and surrounding safety zone.

moments.name: Activate Tapping Pulpit
moments.description: Turn the local control switch ON using FurnaceBreakerKey01 to activate the tapping pulpit control panel.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now, approach the control console near the EAF tapping pulpit. You'll see the FurnaceBreakerKey01 switch at waist height. Your task is to turn the local control switch to ON to activate the tapping pulpit. This will power up the control panel and enable manual operation of the tapping process.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent! The local control switch is now ON. Notice how the control panel has lit up, indicating that the tapping pulpit is now active and ready for manual operation. You now have control over the tapping sequence.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"That's not quite right. Look for the FurnaceBreakerKey01 switch on the control console. You need to rotate it fully to the ON position.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to grab the FurnaceBreakerKey01 switch and rotate it clockwise to the maximum position to turn it ON. The green outline shows you which switch to use.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Let me guide you. Reach out and firmly grasp the FurnaceBreakerKey01 switch. Then, rotate it all the way clockwise until it clicks into the ON position. You'll feel it lock into place when you've reached the maximum rotation.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect work! The tapping pulpit is now fully activated and under local control. You're ready to proceed with the next step of the tapping procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Steel plant control console area with illuminated control panel, FurnaceBreakerKey01 switch in ON position, industrial lighting highlighting the activated tapping pulpit controls.

moments.name: Move Ladle to Tapping Position
moments.description: Use the remote controller to position the ladle under the EBT for tapping.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to position the ladle for tapping. Pick up the remote controller and press the EAF tapping position button to move the ladle under the EBT.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Now locate and press the EAF tapping position button on the remote controller.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Button pressed. The ladle is now moving into tapping position under the EBT.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect. The ladle is now positioned correctly under the EBT, ready for tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"That's not correct. You need to grab the remote controller first, then press the EAF tapping position button.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, pick up the remote controller and look for the EAF tapping position button.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Let me guide you. Grab the remote controller with either hand, then squeeze the trigger to press the EAF tapping position button.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The ladle is in position and we're ready for the next step in the tapping procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF control area with operator holding remote controller, ladle transfer car visible in background moving into position beneath the furnace tapping point

moments.name: Position Material Addition Chute
moments.description: Use the remote controller to move the RCH01 selector to CCW position for tapping.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now, use the gunning and fettling remote controller to position the material addition chute. Move the RCH01 selector to the CCW position to align the chute for the tapping procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The material addition chute is now in the correct tapping position. The chute is aligned and ready for the steel tapping procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember to use the remote controller to move the RCH01 selector to the CCW position. This will position the chute correctly.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Grab the gunning and fettling remote controller and press the selector switch to move RCH01 to the CCW position. This action positions the chute for tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The material addition chute is now positioned. Let's proceed to the next critical step in the tapping procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel mill environment with the gunning and fettling remote control panel in the foreground, material addition chute visible in the background moving into tapping position, ambient industrial lighting with safety markings visible.

moments.name: Verify Bath Conditions
moments.description: Player observes bath temperature and chemistry values being verified before tapping.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Before tapping, we must verify the bath conditions. The target bath temperature must be between 1600 and 1650 degrees Celsius, and the chemistry values must be within the specified ranges for carbon, phosphorus, and sulfur. These parameters ensure the steel quality meets the required specifications. Let's check the current readings on the monitoring system.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: TextMediaAction
actions.Data:
{"content":"Bath Temperature Target: 1600-1650°C
Carbon: Within spec
Phosphorus: Within spec
Sulfur: Within spec","waitForCompletion":false}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Bath conditions verified. All parameters are within acceptable ranges. We can now proceed with the tapping sequence.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel furnace with glowing interior visible, digital display panel showing temperature and chemistry readings in foreground, player observing from safe distance in dimly lit facility.

moments.name: Open EBT and Begin Tapping
moments.description: Operator interacts with furnace breaker key to lift electrodes and initiate tapping sequence.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now you will open the EBT to begin tapping. First, use the furnace breaker key to lift the electrodes, which will enable furnace tilt control. Locate the breaker key at the control panel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. You have grabbed the furnace breaker key. Now rotate it to lift the electrodes.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent! The furnace breaker key is now in position. The electrodes are lifting, and tilt control is enabled. Tapping has begun.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to rotate the furnace breaker key fully to lift the electrodes and begin tapping. Make sure you turn it all the way.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The furnace breaker key at the control panel must be rotated completely. Grab it and turn it to the maximum position to lift the electrodes.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Well done. The EBT is now open and tapping has successfully begun. Molten steel is flowing into the ladle. You will now proceed to monitor the tapping process.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel foundry control panel with furnace breaker key highlighted, large electric arc furnace with rising electrodes in background, molten steel beginning to flow into waiting ladle below, dramatic orange glow illuminating the operator area.

moments.name: Add Ferroalloys During Tapping
moments.description: Interact with ferroalloy hopper to open slide gates when 20-25 tons have been tapped into the ladle.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"As the tapping process continues, you need to add ferroalloys to the ladle. When 20 to 25 tons of molten steel have been tapped, interact with the ferroalloy hopper control to open the slide gates. This allows the ferroalloys to flow into the ladle, ensuring proper alloy composition in the final steel product.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent! The slide gates are now open. The ferroalloys are flowing into the ladle, mixing with the molten steel to achieve the desired chemical composition. This is a critical step in the tapping process to ensure the final steel meets quality specifications.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to interact with the ferroalloy hopper control to open the slide gates. Look for the highlighted hopper and use the control mechanism to release the materials.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The ferroalloy hopper is directly in front of you. Grab or interact with the control to open the slide gates and allow the ferroalloys to flow into the ladle. This must be done at the correct time during tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Well done. The ferroalloys have been successfully added to the ladle. The tapping process continues with proper alloy composition being maintained. Let's proceed to the next step.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel plant ferroalloy hopper station with highlighted control mechanism, molten steel flowing into ladle below, worker preparing to activate slide gates during tapping operation

moments.name: Control Slag Carryover
moments.description: Operator tilts furnace back to deslagging side to control slag carryover when approximately 5-7 tons of steel remain.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"As the ladle reaches approximately 5 to 7 tons of steel remaining, it's critical to tilt the furnace back toward the deslagging side. This controls slag carryover and prevents contamination of the final steel. Use the furnace tilting key to begin the reverse tilt.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. The furnace is tilting back to the deslagging side. This reverse tilt prevents slag from entering the ladle and ensures clean steel quality.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Focus on the furnace tilting key. You need to use it to tilt the furnace back to the deslagging side to control slag carryover.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to grab the furnace tilting key and activate it to tilt the furnace back. This prevents slag from contaminating the steel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Locate the furnace tilting key in front of you. Grab it with either hand, then squeeze the grip button to activate the reverse tilt to the deslagging side.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work controlling the slag carryover. By tilting back to the deslagging side, you've ensured the final steel remains clean and uncontaminated. Let's proceed to finalize the tapping process.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel furnace facility with operator at control console, EAF furnace visibly tilting back toward deslagging side, ladle partially filled with molten steel glowing orange, FurnaceTiltingKey controller in operator's hand, ambient industrial lighting with safety barriers visible.

moments.name: EBT Cleanup and Sand Filling
moments.description: Player observes the final EBT cleanup and sand filling operations after tapping is complete.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"After tapping is complete, the EBT cleanup and sand filling process begins. This critical step ensures the tap hole is properly sealed and prepared for the next heat cycle.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The EBT system retracts and cleans the tap hole area, removing any residual slag or steel. Sand is then injected to seal the hole completely, preventing heat loss and maintaining furnace integrity.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"This completes the steel tapping procedure. The furnace is now ready for the next melting cycle. Always ensure all safety protocols are followed and the area is clear before beginning a new operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. You have successfully completed the EAF steel tapping procedure training. Remember, precision and safety are paramount in every step of this critical operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial electric arc furnace environment post-tapping with EBT system performing cleanup operations and sand filling process, mechanical sounds echoing in the facility

moments.name: Introduction and Safety Briefing
moments.userAction: narration
moments.successHint: auto-advance

moments.name: Reset Steel Weight

moments.successHint: Display shows '0 tons', toast message 'Weight reset complete'
moments.spatialContext: RemoteController at control panel

moments.name: Position Material Addition Chute

moments.successHint: Chute animation moves to tapping position, voice-over 'Material addition chute positioned'

moments.name: Clean Slag Door
moments.userAction: Use GunningAndFettling_Remote to clean slag door ensuring fettling material not higher than 10-15cm

moments.name: Emergency Procedure Review
moments.userAction: narration
moments.successHint: auto-advance

moments.userAction: Touch EmergencyButton to acknowledge location

moments.name: Training Complete Summary
moments.userAction: narration
moments.successHint: auto-advance
moments.spatialContext: Final summary of key safety and operational points

objectives: Safely tap molten steel from EAF into ladles
Achieve required bath temperature and chemical composition before tapping
Maintain proper ladle temperature (1630°C +/- 10°C for ladles in circulation, 1650°C +/- 10°C for new ladles)
Control tapping process to avoid slag carryover into ladle
Manage ferroalloy and slag forming material additions during tapping
Operate EBT (Eccentric Bottom Tapping) system correctly
Ensure proper ladle preparation and inspection before tapping
Execute tapping under arcing procedures when applicable
Complete emergency tapping procedures using emergency hydraulic controls
procedures: Inspect ladle condition and verify temperature is 1000°C to 1200°C
Verify ladle top ring is cleaned and level after filling nozzle sand
Ensure ferroalloys and slag forming materials are ready in bunkers
Turn on local control switch from EAF tapping pulpit to ON position
Verify ladle preheater is in parking position
Move ladle transfer car to tapping position by pressing EAF tapping position button
Reset steel weight to zero before tapping
Move material addition chute to tapping position by moving selector switch RCH01 to CCW
Verify required bath temperature and oxygen levels are achieved
Verify chemical composition is at required level (C%, P, Cu, O2 ppm)
Unlock furnace frame locks
Verify DRI movable chute is working properly
Enable tilting control by rotating TILT-ENABLE selector switch to ON position
Start ladle stirring by selecting gas type (Nitrogen or Argon) and increasing rinsing gas pressure using STIR Line 1 and STIR Line 2 switches
Unlock EBT flap by pressing Extra Stroke By-Pass button and EBT Unlock button simultaneously
Open EBT flap by pressing Extra Stroke By-Pass button and EBT Opening button simultaneously
Observe EBT sand dropping and metal flow from furnace into ladle
If steel doesn't flow, use Oxygen lance to open EBT while wearing Aluminum jacket
When 20-25 tons tapped, add ferroalloys by pressing BIN SHP01 and SHP02 Slide Gate open buttons
Add FeMn, FeSi, SiMn, CaO, CaF2 and other materials as required for steel grade
Manually add recarburizer during ferroalloy addition if needed
Move material addition chute to parking position using selector switch RCH01 to CW
For tapping under arcing: maintain bath temperature during feeding and tapping simultaneously
For tapping under arcing: open furnace main circuit breaker and lift electrodes to tapping position after 20-25 tons tapped
For tapping without arcing: open furnace main circuit breaker, lift electrodes to tapping position and lock before starting tapping
Tilt furnace gradually using joystick to control tilting and complete tapping
When approximately 5-7 tons remain to desired weight (aim 150-155 tons), tilt furnace back to de-slagging side
Move ladle from below furnace by pressing ladle car EAF PREHEAT CHARG.POSITION button
Check ladle stirring availability
Tilt furnace to tapping position until 7° angle is reached
Clean EBT bottom by closing and opening flap using EXTRA STROKE BY-PASS and OPENING-CLOSING buttons
Close EBT flap and lock by pressing LOCK button under EBT movement label
Open EBT cover by pressing EBT COVER OPEN button
Inspect tap hole using camera through monitoring display
Fill EBT with sand by pressing EBT SAND CHARGING OPEN button
Ensure sand forms dome-like pile over tap hole
Close sand hopper by pressing EBT SAND CHARGING CLOSE button
Close EBT cover by pressing EBT COVER CLOSE button
Estimate hot heel quantity after every heat (average should be around 30 tons)
Tilt furnace to horizontal position using joystick
Clean slag door ensuring fettling material is not higher than 10-15 cm above slag apron
Insert shell verticality locks by pressing LOCK button under FRAME label if scrap or electrode charging needed
For emergency: use emergency stick on left side to open hydraulic line by pushing forward continuously
For emergency: use emergency stick on right side to tilt furnace forward for tapping or backward for de-slagging
sopContext.constraints: Use only tools and equipment in good condition and appropriate for the task. All protective and safety devices, machine guards and alarms must be functional. Follow all area safety warning signs and instructions. Maintain proper housekeeping. Report hazards, near-misses, or incidents immediately to Supervisor. Any member can stop activity if unsafe conditions are found and must immediately inform shift Supervisor. Operator shall use all proper PPE during tapping. Do not charge any material in the ladle before tapping. Ladle must not exceed 3 minutes under EAF before start tapping to avoid excessive temperature drop. Do not add recarburizer before adding ferroalloys material as it will lead to boiling. Addition of alloys and recarburizer on the bottom of the ladle is not allowed once it can boil. Stay at least 10 feet radius from suspended loads. Ensure floor is clean and free from obstructions. EBT needs to be changed or repaired by steel pipe if tapping time is equal to or less than 2.5 minutes. Tapping under arcing can be performed during charging hot DRI only. Projected EBT tapping time must be above 3 minutes for tapping under arcing. Furnace must be kept in horizontal position during tapping under arcing. Tapping under arcing should not exceed more than 25% of molten steel inside ladle (40-ton maximum). Ladle addition chute should be moved to parking position before reaching 2° tilting angle. Furnace frame locks must be unlocked before tapping. Only trained and authorized persons must operate equipment. Oxygen lance must have back fire arrestor and holder must always be pressed if pipe is inserted inside tap hole. Lancing pipe must be replaced after each time use. Lancing EBT from upside is not allowed. Control tilting smoothly using joystick to avoid liquid metal contact with water cooled panels. When approximately 5-7 tons remain to desired tapping weight (aim 150-155 tons), furnace must be tilted back to de-slagging side to avoid slag carryover. Minimum free board for ladle is around 400-500 mm. Average hot heel should be around 30 tons. Slag door fettling material should not be higher than 10-15 cm above slag apron. Use emergency stick with extra care and control smoothly to avoid jerking which can lead to electrode breakages. For any emergency, call 02 507 3000 and follow Department Emergency Response Plan SMD 2-PR-002.
